He was from Normandy, and his family continued to hold lordships there. (Further disc …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Peter Piers De Valognes於1048年出生在法國巴-諾門迪曼其傑爾布-可迪維爾瓦洛斯時，他的父親Enguerrand II de Ponthieu11045歲，母親Adelaide de Normandie20歲。他於1074年與Albreda De Ries Saint Sauveur在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1076年住在法國諾門迪曼其瓦洛斯。他去世於1121年在英國英格蘭素福爾可奧福德享年73歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可賓納姆。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Piers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Regular medieval vernacular form of Peter (from the Old French nominative case, as against the oblique Pier, modern Pierre). In the form Pierce it survived into the 18th century, although in part this may be a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name. Piers was revived in the mid-20th century, perhaps partly under the influence of William Langland's great rambling medieval poem Piers Plowman ( 1367–86 ), in which the character of Piers symbolizes the virtues of hard work, honesty, and fairness.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Peter Piers De Valognes was born in 1048, in Valognes, Cherbourg-Octeville, Manche, Lower Normandy, France, his father, Enguerrand II de Ponthieu, was 11045 and his mother, Adelaide de Normandie, was 20. He married Albreda De Ries Saint Sauveur in 1074, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He lived in Valognes, Manche, Normandy, France in 1076. He died in 1121, in Orford, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73, and was buried in Binham, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Peter Piers De Valognes est né en 1048, à Valognes, Cherbourg-Octeville, Manche, Basse-Normandie, France, son père, Enguerrand II de Ponthieu, avait 11045 ans et sa mère, Adelaide de Normandie, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Albreda De Ries Saint Sauveur en 1074, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il a vécu à Valognes, Manche, Normandie, France en 1076. Il est décédé en 1121, à Orford, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Binham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Peter Piers De Valognes 1048 in Valognes, Cherbourg-Octeville, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Enguerrand II de Ponthieu 11045 und seine Mutter Adelaide de Normandie 20. Er hat 1074 in Suffolk, England Albreda De Ries Saint Sauveur geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1076 hat er in Valognes, Manche, Normandie, Frankreich gelebt. Er starb 1121 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Orford, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Binham, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Peter Piers De Valognes è nato il nel 1048 nel Valognes, Cherbourg-Octeville, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Enguerrand II de Ponthieu, aveva 11045 anni e il suo madre, Adelaide de Normandie, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Albreda De Ries Saint Sauveur nel 1074, nel Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Vissuto a Valognes, Manche, Normandie, Francia nel 1076. Morì nel 1121, nel Orford, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolto a Binham, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Peter Piers De Valognesが1048年にフランスバセ-ノーマンディマンチェチェルボウルグ-オクテヴィルヴァログンスで生まれた時，父Enguerrand II de Ponthieuは11045歳，母Adelaide de Normandieは20歳であった。1074年にイギリススフォルクでAlbreda De Ries Saint Sauveurと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1076年にはフランスノーマンディマンチェヴァログンスに住んでいた。彼は1121年にイギリスイギリススフォルクオルフォードで73歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクビンハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Peter Piers De Valognes(이)가 1048년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 먼체 처보우르그-옥테빌 발그네스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Enguerrand II de Ponthieu(은)는 11045세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelaide de Normandie(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1074년 영국 수폴크에서 Albreda De Ries Saint Sauveur(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1076년도에 프랑스 노먼디 먼체 발그네스에서 살았다. 그는 1121년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 르퍼드에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 빈함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Peter Piers De Valognes nasceu em 1048, em Valognes, Cherbourg-Octeville, Manche, Basse-Normandie, França, seu pai, Enguerrand II de Ponthieu, tinha 11045 anos e sua mãe, Adelaide de Normandie, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Albreda De Ries Saint Sauveur em 1074, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele viveu em Valognes, Manche, Normandie, França em 1076. Ele faleceu em 1121, em Orford, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 73 anos, e foi sepultado em Binham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Peter Piers De Valognes родился в 1048 г. в местности валогнесь, чирбург-октевилл, манч, бассе-норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Enguerrand II de Ponthieu было 11045 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelaide de Normandie, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Albreda De Ries Saint Sauveur в 1074 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он жил в местности валогнесь, манч, норманди, Франция в 1076 году. Он умер в 1121 г. в местности орфорд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности бинам, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Peter Piers De Valognes nació en 1048, en Valognes, Cherbourg-Octeville, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Enguerrand II de Ponthieu, tenía 11045 años y su mamá, Adelaide de Normandie, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Albreda De Ries Saint Sauveur en 1074, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Vivió en Valognes, Manche, Normandie, Francia en 1076. Murió en 1121, en Orford, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultado en Binham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
